featured.title: New Mirai campaign exploits RCE flaw in EoL D-Link routers
featured.summary: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting CVE-2025-29635, a high-severity command-injection vulnerability affecting D-Link DIR-823X routers, to enlist devices into the botnet. [...]
featured.suggestedTitle: Mirai Campaign Exploits RCE in D-Link Routers
featured.suggestedSummary: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability, CVE-2025-29635, in end-of-life D-Link routers. This high-severity flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands on affected devices, potentially leading to a large-scale botnet formation. Security teams should ensure these routers are decommissioned or replaced to mitigate this threat.

featured.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
featured.summary:
Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of 9.1 out of 10.0. It's rated Important in severity. An anonymous researcher has been credited with discovering and reporting the flaw.

featured.suggestedTitle: Emergency Patch for Critical ASP.NET Core Flaw Released
featured.suggestedSummary: Microsoft has issued out-of-band security updates to address a critical privilege escalation vulnerability in ASP.NET Core, identified as CVE-2026-40372. The flaw, which affects both macOS and Linux systems, allows attackers to gain elevated privileges through improper authentication handling. Organizations are urged to apply these patches immediately to protect their systems from potential exploitation.

featured.title: France's national agency for managing IDs and passports suffered a data breach last week - Engadget
featured.summary: France's national agency for managing IDs and passports suffered a data breach last week  Engadget
featured.reason: Major data breach in government agency
featured.suggestedTitle: French Government Agency Suffers Massive Data Breach
featured.suggestedSummary: France's national agency for managing IDs and passports has confirmed a data breach, with hackers claiming to have stolen 19 million records. The breach reportedly includes sensitive information from both individual and professional accounts. Security teams should be aware of potential phishing campaigns and identity theft attempts stemming from this incident.

quickNews.title: Apple fixes iOS bug that retained deleted notification data
quickNews.summary: Apple has released out-of-band security updates for iPhone and iPad devices to fix a Notification Services flaw that could allow notifications marked for deletion to remain stored on the device. [...]

quickNews.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
quickNews.summary: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.

quickNews.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
quickNews.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository.

quickNews.title: New npm supply-chain attack self-spreads to steal auth tokens
quickNews.summary: A new supply chain attack targeting the Node Package Manager (npm) ecosystem is stealing developer credentials and attempting to spread through packages published from compromised accounts. [...]
